'''Will Lovell''' (born 10 May 1993) is a [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|sr}} forward for the [[London Broncos]] in the [[RFL Championship|Betfred Championship]].

He played for the [[London Broncos|Broncos]] in the [[Super League]] between 2012 and 2014, and has spent time on loan from them at the [[London Skolars]] in the [[League 1 (rugby league)|League 1]]. He has also spent the [[2015 Championship 1|2015]] and [[2016 League 1|2016]] seasons at the [[London Skolars|Skolars]]. Earlier in his career he played as a {{rlp|ce}}.

==Background==
Lovell was born in [[Northampton]], [[Northamptonshire, England]].

He was graduated from [[St Mary's University, Twickenham]].<ref>[https://www.stmarys.ac.uk/news/2020/st-marys-alumnus-will-lovell-talks-about-his-rugby-journey St Mary’s Alumnus Will Lovell Talks about his Rugby Journey]</ref>

==Career==
===Early career===
Lovell played [[rugby union]] as a youth and was in the junior system of the [[Northampton Saints]].<ref>[https://www.northamptonchron.co.uk/news/people/northampton-born-will-lovell-named-captain-of-rugby-league-team-london-broncos-1361696 Northampton born Will Lovell named captain of rugby league team London Broncos]</ref> 

He also played [[rugby league]] as an amateur for the [[Northampton Demons]] and from there progressed through to the London Broncos Academy system in 2010 and 2011.

===London Broncos===
Lovell made his [[Super League]] debut for the London Broncos against the [[Catalans Dragons]] at the [[Etihad Stadium]] in May 2012.<ref>[https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12196/11908505/will-lovell-new-london-broncos-captains-crazy-career-route Will Lovell: New London Broncos captain's 'crazy' career route]</ref>

He played one game on loan at the [[London Skolars]] in the [[League 1 (rugby league)|League 1]] in 2013.

He spent time on loan from the Broncos at the [[London Skolars|Skolars]] in the [[League 1 (rugby league)|League 1]] in 2014.

===London Skolars===
Lovell joined the London Skolars ahead of the [[2015 Championship 1]] season. He spent two seasons at [[White Hart Lane Community Sports Centre]], featuring prominently in both 2015 and 2016.{{citation needed|date=March 2023}}
[[File:Will Lovell.jpg|thumb|right|125px|Lovell playing for the London Skolars in 2015]]

===Return to the Broncos===
After a successful spell in North London, Lovell stepped up a division and signed a three-month deal with his former club ahead of the 2017 season. He was injured and took on a community role in lieu of his playing contract being extended.{{citation needed|date=February 2024}}

He returned to the club as a player ahead of the 2018 season, playing mainly as a {{rlp|sr}} forward. He played a pivotal role in the Broncos' successful 2018 season, the culmination of which was their 4-2 win over the [[Toronto Wolfpack]] in the [[Million Pound Game]] to earn promotion to Super League in 2019.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/toronto-vs-london/50049 |title=Toronto 2-4 London: Broncos shock Wolfpack to earn 2019 Super League place |website=Sky Sports |date=8 October 2018 |access-date=16 March 2026}}</ref>
[[File:Will Lovell London Broncos.jpg|thumb|215px|right|Lovell playing for the London Broncos in 2019]]
Following the clubs relegation from the [[Super League]] Lovell was named captain of the club ahead of the 2020 season, and also returned to playing as a {{rlp|ce}}.{{citation needed|date=March 2023}}

In June 2022 Lovell suffered a suspected fractured neck against [[Workington Town]] at [[Derwent Park]]. He was stretchered off and then rushed to hospital in an ambulance. He missed only one match, with the injury not being as bad as initially feared.<ref>[https://cumbriacrack.com/2022/06/03/broncos-captain-suffers-major-injury-in-town-game/ Broncos captain suffers major injury in Town game]</ref>

On 15 October 2023, Lovell played in the London Broncos upset [[Million Pound Game]] victory over [[Toulouse Olympique]].<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/67105947|title=Championship Grand Final: Toulouse 14-18 London Broncos - Capital club back in Super League|work=BBC Sport |date=15 October 2023 }}</ref>

Lovell's long-held role as captain was handed to newcomer [[Reagan Campbell-Gillard]] ahead of the [[2026 RFL Championship season]].<ref>[https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/london-broncos-name-2026-captain-as-real-leader-appointed London Broncos name 2026 captain as ‘real leader’ appointed]</ref>
[[File:Will London London Broncos 2026.jpg|thumb|215px|right|Lovell playing for the London Broncos in 2026]]
On 15 March 2026 he played his 200th career game for the London Broncos, in their [[2026_RFL_1895_Cup#Preliminary_round|1895 Cup Preliminary Round]] victory over the [[Barrow Raiders]] at [[Athletic Ground, Richmond|Richmond's Athletic Ground]].<ref>[https://www.londonbroncosrl.com/match-report-barrow-raiders#:~:text=Skipper%20Will%20Lovell%20marked%20his,Teare%20near%20the%20try%20line. Match Report: Barrow Raiders]</ref><ref>[https://www.totalrl.com/london-broncos-too-strong-for-barrow-raiders-in-1895-cup/ London Broncos too strong for Barrow Raiders in 1895 Cup]</ref>

==Personal life==
Lovell is a qualified [[Physical Education|PE]] teacher. He combines his rugby league training and playing duties with teaching commitments at [[Milbourne Lodge School]].<ref>[https://www.swlondoner.co.uk/sport/19012023-london-broncos-captain-will-lovell-targets-play-offs-and-return-to-super-league London Broncos captain Will Lovell targets play-offs and return to Super League]</ref>
